desc: Ghent is a canal-laced Flemish city with medieval skyline views, strong food culture, and easy rail links across Belgium.
hls: Monthly climate fields are imported from the shared climate CSV and verified against source rows.
Shoulder seasons typically provide the best comfort-to-crowd trade-off for most visitors.
Peak-season periods raise crowding and accommodation pressure, so booking ahead helps.

todo.name: Historic core walk
todo.desc: Start with an orientation walk through the most characterful central districts.

todo.name: Top local food stop
todo.desc: Plan one meal around regional specialties in a well-reviewed local venue.

todo.name: Half-day nature or coast outing
todo.desc: Keep one flexible half-day for a nearby scenic area or waterfront route.

wifi.note: City-center accommodations and cafés are usually dependable; confirm speed if you need to work remotely.

prac.fltNote: Ghent is usually easiest via Brussels Airport, then train or car onward (around 1 hour).
prac.lang: Dutch and French are the main languages in Ghent; English is commonly understood in visitor-facing venues.
prac.tz: CET (UTC+1) in winter, CEST (UTC+2) in summer.
prac.visa: Belgium follows Schengen policy; many non-EU tourists can stay up to 90 days in a 180-day window visa-free, while others need a Schengen visa before travel.
alerts: Hotel demand rises during the Gentse Feesten period; reserve central stays well ahead.
Rain showers can be frequent year-round; pack waterproof layers for canal walks.
Cycling traffic is heavy in the core; use marked pedestrian zones and crossing lights.

sweet: May-September usually offers the best mix of longer daylight and comfortable city-walking weather.
